Off Calendar Aug-23-2016 Off Calendar

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Continued To Sep-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with (trustee) Drexel Bradshaw.

Ms. Robello stated that the trust was under court supervision to fund the trust, that her client is the successor trustee, that the previous order in the conservatorship matter stated that the trust need not be under court supervision following the first accounting, that they have filed a supplement to the accounting as to why it should not be under court supervision, that there is an additional cost to the conservatee for bond and accountings which would not be incurred if the trust was not under court supervision, that there is nothing further to warrant court supervision and that they have sent notices to the beneficiaries, that 2 of the 5 beneficiaries are deceased, that one has appeared in court and that there are no objections. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-01-2015 Continued To Jul-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with (trustee) Drexel Bradshaw.

Ms. Robello reported that the declinations had been filed in the conservatoship matter, have now been refiled in the trust matter, that all issues in the examiner's notes have been resolved, that there was a typographical error in the petition, that a revised order has been submitted and that it is fine to waive the $3.45. Ms. Robello also stated that this trust matter was not initially under court supervision, that this matter was opened to fund the trust, that it is now appropriate to have this trust revert back to not being under court supervision and that the previous order stated that court supervision would be removed after the first accounting. The court stated that it is not inclined to remove the trust from court supervision due to the conservatorship and stated that the examiner's notes indicate that bond should be increased. Ms. Robello stated that an additional bond will not be necessary if the trust is no longer under court supervision and that if there is a change of mind regarding the trust remaining under court supervision would like a chance to submit a supplemental document regarding the expenses that this will incur, agreeing to provide the supplemental submission no later that June 29, 2015 at which time the matter will be deemed submitted.

**7/1/15**
At the request of Ms. Robello the matter has been continued to July 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
